variable_name: Bioclimatic variables (19 vars: temperature, precipitation seasonality, extremes)
citation: Fick, S.E. & Hijmans, R.J. (2017). WorldClim 2: new 1km spatial resolution climate surfaces for global land areas. International Journal of Climatology, 37(12), 4302–4315.
preprocessing_notes: Resample to 1 km COG, clip to AOI. BIO1=Annual Mean Temp; BIO12=Annual Precipitation; BIO15=Precipitation Seasonality most relevant for agroforestry suitability.
known_limitations: 30-year climatological mean; does not capture interannual variability or extremes. Resolution may mask local topographic effects.
do_not_use_for: Site-level microclimate analysis or real-time weather monitoring

variable_name: High-resolution bioclimatic variables including temperature extremes and precipitation
citation: Karger, D.N. et al. (2017). Climatologies at high resolution for the earth's land surface areas (CHELSA). Scientific Data, 4, 170122.
preprocessing_notes: Resample to 1 km COG. CHELSA-BIOCLIM+ Version 1.2 used by Yadav et al. (2024) for agroforestry suitability in Punjab. Use Bio1 (Annual Mean Temp), Bio12 (Annual Precipitation), Tmax_Jan, Tmax_May.
known_limitations: Statistical downscaling from ERA5; may show artefacts in complex terrain. Less validated over Africa and Central Asia.
do_not_use_for: Short-term weather forecasting or sub-field precision agriculture

variable_name: Projected bioclimatic variables under moderate emissions scenario
citation: Karger, D.N. et al. (2023). CHELSA-W5E5: Daily 1 km meteorological forcing data for climate impact studies. Earth System Science Data, 15, 2445–2464.
preprocessing_notes: Use delta-change approach relative to baseline. Clip to project AOI. Ensemble mean of available CMIP6 GCMs preferred.
known_limitations: Ensemble spread is wide in tropical regions. Precipitation projections carry higher uncertainty than temperature.
do_not_use_for: Single-GCM deterministic planning; local infrastructure design

citation: Vicente-Serrano, S.M. et al. (2010). A Multiscalar Drought Index Sensitive to Global Warming: The Standardized Precipitation Evapotranspiration Index. Journal of Climate, 23(7), 1696–1718.
preprocessing_notes: Extract SPEI-12 (12-month accumulation window) for drought risk. Compute mean and trend over 1981–2010 baseline. Bilinearly resample to 1 km for overlay.
known_limitations: Very coarse native resolution (0.5°). Misses sub-regional drought heterogeneity. PET estimation method affects values.
do_not_use_for: Farm-level drought monitoring; flash drought detection

variable_name: Soil properties: pH, organic carbon, clay, sand, CEC, bulk density, nitrogen
variable_unit: multiple (pH: unitless; SOC: g/kg; clay/sand: %; CEC: cmolc/kg; BD: kg/dm³; N: cg/kg)
citation: Poggio, L. et al. (2021). SoilGrids 2.0: producing soil information for the globe with quantified spatial uncertainty. SOIL, 7, 217–240.
preprocessing_notes: Extract layers at 0–30 cm depth (topsoil) and 30–60 cm (subsoil). Resample to 1 km. pH, SOC, clay content, CEC are primary agroforestry suitability constraints per Ahmad et al. (2020), Chuma et al. (2021), Haile (2024).
known_limitations: Prediction uncertainty is high in data-sparse regions (much of sub-Saharan Africa). Point observations biased toward accessible areas. Temporal static — does not capture land-use change effects on soil.
do_not_use_for: Precision soil management at plot level; nutrient management plans without ground-truthing

variable_name: Soil texture class, drainage, nutrient status, available water capacity
citation: FAO/IIASA/ISRIC/ISSCAS/JRC (2023). Harmonized World Soil Database version 2.0. FAO, Rome, Italy & IIASA, Laxenburg, Austria.
preprocessing_notes: Used by Ahmad et al. (2020) as primary soil input for South Asia agroforestry suitability. Join soil mapping unit attributes on raster values. Extract topsoil properties for suitability scoring.
known_limitations: Coarser conceptual resolution than SoilGrids despite same pixel size — many large mapping units contain internally variable soils. Outdated in rapidly changing agricultural areas.
do_not_use_for: Detailed site characterisation; soil carbon accounting

citation: Farr, T.G. et al. (2007). The Shuttle Radar Topography Mission. Reviews of Geophysics, 45(2), RG2004.
preprocessing_notes: Derive slope (degrees) and aspect from DEM in GEE. Slope is primary biophysical constraint for agroforestry machinery access and erosion risk. Used across nearly all 23 AF papers in stocktake (Ahmad et al. 2017, 2018, 2020; Chuma et al. 2021; Haile 2024; Nath et al. 2021; Mushtaq et al. 2023; Seja et al. 2022; Wotlolan et al. 2021).
known_limitations: Voids in high-relief areas filled with ASTER or other interpolation methods — check for artefacts. Vegetation canopy not filtered — potential overestimation of elevation under forest cover.
do_not_use_for: Hydraulic modelling requiring bare-earth DEM; landslide hazard at plot scale

citation: Zanaga, D. et al. (2022). ESA WorldCover 10m 2021 v200. Zenodo.
preprocessing_notes: Reclassify to agricultural mask (cropland, grassland, sparse vegetation) to define eligible agroforestry conversion area. Exclude built-up, water bodies, bare soil with high salinity. Aggregate to 100m or 1km for pipeline. Used as LULC base by multiple AF studies.
known_limitations: Mixed-pixel confusion in fragmented agricultural landscapes. Annual snapshot — does not capture seasonal cultivation. Accuracy lower in arid landscapes and under cloud cover.
do_not_use_for: Crop-type mapping; sub-field land use classification

dataset_name: Global Aridity Index and PET v3 (CGIAR-CSI)
citation: Trabucco, A. & Zomer, R. (2019). Global Aridity Index and Potential Evapotranspiration (ET0) Climate Database v2. CGIAR-CSI GeoPortal.
preprocessing_notes: AI < 0.5 = dryland; used to mask unsuitable extreme arid zones for agroforestry. Ahmad et al. (2020) use aridity index as key suitability layer for South Asia. Nath et al. (2021) use Global Aridity Index for Himalayan region.
known_limitations: Derived from WorldClim 1970–2000 climatology — does not reflect recent drying trends. PET estimated with Penman-Monteith assuming reference crop.
do_not_use_for: Real-time drought monitoring; current water stress assessment

citation: UNEP-WCMC & IUCN (2024). Protected Planet: The World Database on Protected Areas (WDPA). Cambridge: UNEP-WCMC and IUCN.
preprocessing_notes: Rasterize to 100m. Hard exclusion mask for IUCN categories Ia, Ib, II for agroforestry suitability (conversion not permitted). Categories V, VI may allow agroforestry within management plans — flag as conditional. Used by Chuma et al. (2021) as exclusion layer around Itombwe Nature Reserve.
known_limitations: Boundary data quality varies by country. Some protected areas are paper parks with little enforcement. Does not capture community conserved areas (OECMs) comprehensively.
do_not_use_for: Legal compliance decisions; site-level permitting

citation: OpenStreetMap contributors (2024). OpenStreetMap. www.openstreetmap.org.
preprocessing_notes: Compute Euclidean distance to nearest road of any class. Used by Chuma et al. (2021) as infrastructure accessibility layer. Apply 5km threshold as moderate accessibility constraint for agroforestry market access. is_scenario_candidate = true (road investment can change this).
known_limitations: OSM coverage quality is highly variable in rural sub-Saharan Africa, Central Asia, and remote tropical regions — significant under-mapping in key project areas. Validate against national road datasets where possible.
do_not_use_for: Routing or logistics planning; travel-time modelling without validation

citation: WorldPop (2020). Global High Resolution Population Denominators Project - Funded by The Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation (OPP1134076). University of Southampton.
preprocessing_notes: Aggregate to 1 km for pipeline. Use as proxy for population exposure in climate risk and as denominator for opportunity space poverty metrics.
known_limitations: Constrained model relies on settlement/building footprint mapping — may undercount nomadic/dispersed rural populations. Accuracy varies with census quality of input data.
do_not_use_for: Individual household targeting; electoral boundary analysis

dataset_name: FAO Global Soil Organic Carbon Map (GSOCmap) v1.6.1
citation: FAO & ITPS (2018). Global Soil Organic Carbon Map (GSOCmap) v1.6.1. Technical Report. FAO, Rome.
preprocessing_notes: Use as carbon co-benefit layer in opportunity space. Mendonça et al. (2023) use SOC sequestration potential as an agroforestry prioritisation criterion. Kiziridis et al. (2026) map silvopastoral carbon sequestration. Complement with FAO GSOCseq for sequestration rate.
known_limitations: Country submissions vary in quality and methodology. National and sub-national SOC maps (where available) should be used in preference.
do_not_use_for: Carbon crediting baselines without country-specific validation; REDD+ baseline setting

dataset_name: FAO-IIASA Global Agro-Ecological Zones v4 (33-class AEZ)
citation: FAO & IIASA (2021). Global Agro-Ecological Zones – GAEZ v4. FAO, Rome & IIASA, Laxenburg.
preprocessing_notes: Use for AEZ context classification in T7. Key AEZ groups for agroforestry context overrides: humid tropics, sub-humid tropics, semi-arid, highland tropics.
known_limitations: AEZ boundaries are climate-envelope based and do not capture soil or management diversity within zones.
do_not_use_for: Crop yield modelling at field scale

dataset_name: FAO/IFAD Farming Systems of the World (Dixon et al. 2001 / updated)
citation: Dixon, J., Gulliver, A. & Gibbon, D. (2001). Farming Systems and Poverty: Improving Farmers' Livelihoods in a Changing World. FAO and World Bank, Rome & Washington D.C.
preprocessing_notes: Rasterize at 10 km then resample to 1 km with majority resampling. Used to populate T7 farming system contexts and to apply context-specific suitability overrides in T4.
known_limitations: Classification is 20+ years old; agricultural systems have shifted significantly in some regions. Mixed-farming boundaries are expert-delineated and coarse.
do_not_use_for: Subnational farming system analysis without local validation

dataset_name: World Bank Poverty and Inequality Platform — Subnational Poverty Estimates
geographic_coverage: Global (subnational admin-1 or admin-2 where available)
citation: World Bank (2024). Poverty and Inequality Platform (PIP). World Bank Group, Washington DC.
preprocessing_notes: Interpolate to raster using admin boundary weights and WorldPop population density. Combine with spatial poverty maps (e.g. CGIAR CELL5M poverty layers) for higher-resolution estimates.
known_limitations: Sub-national resolution limited to admin-1 or -2 in most countries. Survey years vary. Does not capture within-admin spatial variation.
do_not_use_for: Household targeting; beneficiary selection for project implementation

variable_name: Tree canopy cover (year 2000 baseline) and annual loss/gain
citation: Hansen, M.C. et al. (2013). High-Resolution Global Maps of 21st-Century Forest Cover Change. Science, 342(6160), 850–853.
preprocessing_notes: Current tree cover = baseline (2000) − loss + gain. Used to identify areas below 10% tree cover as agroforestry expansion opportunity and areas with existing tree cover for context. Singh et al. (2024) and Ahmad et al. (2020) use Hansen tree cover as AF suitability input.
known_limitations: Confusion between natural forest and plantation/agroforestry systems. Understory trees in agroforestry are often missed at 30m resolution.
do_not_use_for: Legal deforestation monitoring for jurisdictional REDD+; plot-level timber volume

citation: Fick, S.E. & Hijmans, R.J. (2017). WorldClim 2: new 1km spatial resolution climate surfaces for global land areas. International Journal of Climatology, 37(12), 4302-4315.
preprocessing_notes: Rainfall is the second most frequent input variable (38 of 85 papers). Extract mean annual precipitation and wet-season monthly totals. Use for SCS-CN runoff depth calculation combined with HSG soil map. Buraihi & Shariff (2015) use projected precipitation to assess future RWH viability under climate change.
known_limitations: 30-year climatological mean; interannual variability not captured. Resolution too coarse for localised orographic precipitation in complex terrain.
do_not_use_for: Flood frequency analysis; design-storm estimation for dam sizing

dataset_name: HydroSHEDS Watershed Boundaries and River Network
variable_name: Sub-watershed delineation, stream order, drainage area
citation: Lehner, B. et al. (2008). New global hydrography derived from spaceborne elevation data. Eos, Transactions AGU, 89(10), 93-94.
preprocessing_notes: Use HydroBASINS level 8-10 for sub-watershed delineation context. Stream order used as a primary RWH site-type discriminator: first/second-order streams suit check dams and gully plugs; third/fourth-order streams suit percolation tanks; larger streams suit small dams. Kumar et al. (2018) and Singh et al. (2016) use stream order as a primary MCDA criterion.
known_limitations: Derived from 3-arc-second SRTM; misses ephemeral channels in flat arid terrain. Resolution insufficient for first-order gully mapping.
do_not_use_for: Detailed gully morphometry; flash flood routing

citation: Farr et al. (2007). SRTM. Reviews of Geophysics.
preprocessing_notes: WH module alias for SRTM. See srtm_dem_90m entry for full notes.
known_limitations: Voids in high-relief terrain.
do_not_use_for: Engineering design

citation: Fick & Hijmans (2017). WorldClim 2. International Journal of Climatology.
preprocessing_notes: Extract mean annual precipitation and wet-season monthly totals for SCS-CN runoff calculation.
known_limitations: 30-year climatological mean; interannual variability not captured.
do_not_use_for: Flood frequency analysis; design-storm estimation
